text: Modern web development requires strong attention to security. One essential measure is HTTPS, which encrypts data between the user's browser and the server, preventing attackers from intercepting sensitive information. Authentication systems, like passwords or two-factor verification, ensure only authorized users access protected areas. Equally important is input validation; developers should never trust user-provided data, as malicious input can exploit common vulnerabilities such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS). By validating and sanitizing all inputs, developers reduce these risks. Ultimately, combining HTTPS, robust authentication, and thorough input validation helps protect both users and systems from significant security threats.
